cowardice_flags.avoided: Mature view's 'why public education keeps failing the children' and 'crisis of confidence'
cowardice_flags.should_say: National schools fail because post-NEP policy turned them into Malay-Islamic identity vehicles: heavy religious indoctrination, Bahasa-medium limits, and race-based teacher hiring that prioritizes bumiputera quotas over merit.

cowardice_flags.avoided: Political lens explanation for why the debate persists
cowardice_flags.should_say: Malay-centric parties (UMNO, PAS, even PH) protect the status quo to appease voters and religious lobbies; real reform would require dismantling Ketuanan Melayu and political Islam's curriculum grip.

audience_check: Ipoh taxi driver, Penang hawker, and Johor factory worker feel spoken TO — it validates their daily SK failures, bullying, and scramble for better options. Kota Bharu teacher and Sabah farmer might bristle defensively at the Malay flight data but recognize the quality verdict. They would share the SJKT abandonment and class escape points with friends rather than roll eyes; no elitist lecture, just data confirming what they already know.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Decades of bumiputera entitlements fostered an entitlement mindset where academic mediocrity is cushioned; many segments prioritize religious conformity and identity over competitive excellence.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Risk of racism accusations in Malaysia's hyper-sensitive racial politics.

hard_truths_missing.truth: SJKC succeed through tiger parenting, extra tuition culture, strict discipline, and deliberate parallel ethnic ecosystem that resists full integration into the Malay-centric national system.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Would challenge the minority-victim narrative and imply voluntary segregation for advantage.

hard_truths_missing.truth: SJKT collapse worsened by intra-community caste/class fractures, family instability, social dysfunction, and weaker collective lobbying power than Chinese Dong Zong.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Politically incorrect to highlight internal Indian responsibility alongside state neglect.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Every party (BN, PH, PN) treats education as ethnic vote-bank patronage; teacher unions and lobbies block reform to protect their power bases.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Implicates the entire political ecosystem, not just one side.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Political Islam (distinct from personal faith) has captured national schools with excessive agama content, conservative norms, and dakwah influence, making SK uncompetitive even for moderate Malay parents.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Explosive risk of being branded anti-Islam in a Muslim-majority country.

gender_truths_avoided.truth: Bullying and safety fears hit girls' parents harder, pushing middle-class flight to SJKC/international; boys (especially Malay/Indian) show higher behavioral disengagement and dropout in chaotic, religious-heavy SK environments.
gender_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Forces acknowledgment of sex differences in school experience without fitting progressive or conservative talking points.

class_truths_avoided.truth: Racial policies obscure how lower-class Malays and Indians are trapped in failing national/Tamil schools while middle/upper classes of all races exit; NEP rhetoric hides the creation of an economic underclass.
class_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Exposes Madani governance failures on inequality and shows race framing as a distraction from class apartheid.

predictions.if_nothing_changes: 6 months: 5-10 more SJKT closures, bullying cases up, Malay SJKC enrolment rises further; 2 years: international/private Malaysian enrolment surges 20%, national schools solidify as last-resort for the poor, widened achievement gaps and talent emigration accelerate.
predictions.who_can_fix: Anwar Ibrahim's PH government and MOE leadership
predictions.why_they_wont: Short-term survival depends on not alienating Malay-Muslim voters, teacher unions, or Islamic lobbies — reform invites PAS/UMNO attacks as anti-Bumiputera or anti-Islam.

strongest_card.why: ISEAS survey (69% Malays cite academics, not culture) delivers an irrefutable gut-punch that collapses the entire ethnic debate into a raw quality referendum.

weakest_card.why: Mature view calls for 'honesty' and vague fixes but stays diplomatic, refusing to name Islamization, quotas, or universal political cowardice as the actual barriers.

overall_assessment: Sharp data-led reframe that guts the unity-vs-culture circus and correctly flags national school collapse, SJKT abandonment, and class escape as the real story. Yet it's still too polite and evasive: it stops at symptoms ('quality crisis') without gutting the sacred cows — political Islam's curriculum takeover, NEP-induced mediocrity, and every party's vote-bank cowardice. Real Malaysians already know SK is broken; this analysis describes the flight but flinches from explaining why the system was designed to fail them.
